questions.question: Skin works as a respiratory organ in which of the following Animals ?
questions.explanation: Skin works as a respiratory organ in frog. The frog has three respiratory surfaces on its body which it uses to exchange gas with its surroundings: the skin, in the lungs and on the lining of the mouth.

questions.question: Which of the following type of Kidneys is found in an adult frog?
questions.explanation: Mesonephros is permanent kidney of frog and most fish, developing posterior to and replacing the pronephros of the embryonic and larval stages. It is a paired organ consisting of a set of nephrons having capsules that filter blood from the glomerulus and tubules whose cells reabsorb water and nutrients and secrete nitrogenous wastes.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct feature of a frog?
questions.explanation: Frogs have eyes and they have 3 eyelids. The pinnae are the visible part of the ear that resides on the outside of the head, although they do have a middle ear called the tympanic cavity and an inner ear.

questions.question: Which of the following are characteristic of amphibians?

options.label: A
options.text: two pairs of limbs for locomotion

options.label: C
options.text: live both on land and in water

questions.explanation: Amphibians have two pairs of limbs for locomotion, the heart is three-chambered and they live both on land and in water.

questions.question: Into how many parts are amphibians divided?
questions.explanation: The Amphibians are divided into three orders - Apoda, Urodela, and Anura.
